summary: McKenzie reports on the early metrics of his Bingo Card Creator software business, noting that only 6% of visitors and 10% of paid visitors download a trial. At roughly $1 per trial download, he calculates he needs at least a 5% trial-to-purchase conversion rate to break even. He identifies landing page optimization as the key lever to improve these economics.
key_points: Only 6% of all visitors and 10% of paid traffic converted to trial downloads, establishing a baseline conversion rate
The cost per trial download worked out to approximately $1, setting a clear economic constraint on the business
A 5% trial-to-purchase conversion rate is the minimum threshold needed to make the unit economics work
Landing page optimization is identified as the primary lever for improving the funnel
money_quotes: Since I added my conversion checking code roughly 6% of all visitors (and 10%ish of the ones I pay for) actually download a trial.
To make any money at that rate at all, I need to have a conversion of trial downloads to purchasers above 5%.
I really need to work on my landing pages I think.
key_insight: Early-stage software businesses live or die by their conversion funnels, and understanding the math connecting acquisition cost to trial-to-purchase conversion is essential for viability.
